name: Allstars Of Mecklenburg Inc
recent_news: No web presence or news found; appears to be a very small (3-employee) special-needs transport operator out of a PO Box in Matthews, NC. No school bus or district contracts identified.
fit_notes: Sub-scale operator (3 employees, ~$217K revenue, PO Box only) with no confirmed school district contracts. Not an attractive tuck-in for Summit; likely a private-pay/Medicaid van service rather than yellow school bus contractor.

name: Lundberg Specialty Transport
recent_news: Listed on Charlotte Parent special needs resources directory as a wheelchair-accessible transport provider. No M&A activity or growth news found. Very small operator.
fit_notes: Niche wheelchair/special-needs van service for mobility-limited riders; 3 employees and ~$217K revenue suggests private-pay/NEMT rather than district school bus contracts. Not a meaningful tuck-in for a yellow-bus platform like Summit.

name: Walls Logistics LLC
recent_news: Listed in NPI/healthcare provider databases and Black-owned business directory as a non-emergency medical transport (van) service in Charlotte. Phone 704-750-1554. No school district contracts found.
fit_notes: NEMT van operator, not a school bus contractor. 3 employees, residential address. Not an appropriate tuck-in target for Summit's school-bus platform.

name: Absolute Luxury Limousine LLC
recent_news: No specific web presence located; name suggests luxury/livery limo service rather than school transportation. No school district contracts or special-needs SPED contracts identified.
fit_notes: Appears to be a luxury limousine/livery service, not a school bus or SPED operator. Wrong business model; not a tuck-in candidate for Summit.

name: Affordbladorable Limousine LLC
recent_news: No web presence located under this name (possible misspelling of Affordable Adorable). Aurora, CO special-needs student transportation is dominated by EverDriven and HopSkipDrive contracting with Aurora Public Schools, not small limo LLCs.
fit_notes: Limousine LLC operating from a residential address in Aurora; 4 employees, ~$215K revenue. Wrong vehicle class and operating model for Summit's yellow-bus tuck-in thesis. Not a target.
